bullets.text: Combined ratio of 86.6 for Q2 2006, up only 0.5 points year-over-year; prior accident periods contributed approximately 2 favorable points to the calendar-year combined ratio.

Net premiums written grew 2% for the quarter, below expectations; industry-wide 2005 growth of 0.5% and Q1 2006 estimate of 0.6% confirm broad market softness; management announced intent to modify pricing to stimulate new applications.
Drive auto PIF up only 1% year-over-year; Direct auto PIF up 6%; Commercial Auto grew 9% with a 76.4 combined ratio and an estimated top market share position of 6.8% in commercial auto.

Growing dynamic of online-initiated agency prospects observed; Internet quote volume from web-based agents rising but with significantly lower conversion than traditional agency flow, signaling a structural channel shift.

Internet sales represented 60–65% of Direct new business; Progressive's website topped Keynote WebExcellence rankings in June; new advertising agency evaluation underway to sharpen the Direct brand message.

Concierge claims expansion on schedule with 42 centers in 18 states operational; 11 additional sites expected before year-end; customer loyalty and Net Promoter Score measures being integrated as key performance indicators.

Management less confident it can predict the pace or timing of margin normalization; announced shift to modify pricing trends and make smart moves to stimulate growth rather than waiting for external frequency or severity pressure.
